frozen: ground truth annotated manually by the author after reading the chat, BEFORE writing any extractor or running any A/B; never modified afterwards

active_claims.what: the worker memory leak is caused by a module-level unbounded dict cache keyed by config-path-plus-hash
the cache was named per-task but is effectively process-global
httpx clients hold connection pools if not explicitly closed; possible secondary leak source

active_constraints.what: do not ship the cache fix until httpx leak has been verified — the two could mask each other
do not switch the JSON serializer to orjson now (new C dependency, risk during a leak situation)

decisions.what: fix the cache with functools.lru_cache(maxsize=256) — stdlib, no new dep
ship the lru_cache hotfix today; do httpx verification, memory regression, broader audit in a follow-up branch
drop the orjson direction; keep stdlib json
add memory-budget alerting (RSS > 1GB pages someone) as a follow-up, not blocking on it

open_conflicts.what: ship today (stop OOM bleeding) vs ship right (verify httpx, regression suite, broader audit) — speed vs diligence

open_questions.what: do httpx Client objects in our codebase leak because they are not closed as context managers
are there other module-level mutable containers elsewhere that could leak the same way
